accepted.reason: Interval clustering over sorted endpoints is a strong loop-based algorithmic optimization candidate.
Binary search over cumulative probabilities is a reusable core routine with clear optimization relevance.
3D voxel masking with hull half-space tests is compute-heavy and well aligned with Cython acceleration.
Smolyak coefficient updates are compact numerical kernels with deterministic loop structure.
Bilinear interpolation and mirrored boundary handling are standard image-processing kernels for optimization training.
K-means distance matrix computation is a classic numeric hotspot with clear performance tuning opportunities.

skipped.reason: CEF stream wrapper is integration/memory-management glue rather than a standalone optimization workload.
Relies on SciPy-special bindings and physics-specific context; less suitable as a portable unpaired GRPO Python target.
Puzzle-specific script with file I/O dependency has limited transfer value for optimization training.
Argument marshalling helper is interface glue code, not a computational optimization exemplar.
